master_row.title: Irrotational Magnetic Field Curl Field Equation
master_row.canonical_statement:
egin{theorem}[Magnetic Field Decomposition] Every magnetic field $\vec{B}$ admits unique decomposition:  egin{equation} \vec{B} = \vec{B}_{	ext{irrotational}} + \vec{B}_{	ext{solenoidal}} + \vec{B}_{	ext{harmonic}} \end{equation} where:  egin{align} \vec{B}_{	ext{irrotational}} &= -
abla\Phi_M, \quad 
abla^2\Phi_M = 
abla \cdot \vec{M} \ \vec{B}_{	ext{solenoidal}} &= 
abla 	imes \vec{A}, \quad 
abla^2\vec{A} = -\mu_0
abla 	imes \vec{M} \ \vec{B}_{	ext{harmonic}} &: 
abla \cdot \vec{B}_{	ext{harmonic}} = 0, 
abla 	imes \vec{B}_{	ext{harmonic}} = 0 \end{align} \end{theorem}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Helmholtz-Hodge Decomposition’.
master_row.dictionary: Irrotational Magnetic Field Curl Field Equation — B_irrotational &= -∇Φ_M, ∇^2Φ_M = ∇ · M B_solenoidal &= ∇ × A, ∇^2A = -μ_0∇ × M B_harmonic &: ∇ · B_harmonic = 0, ∇ × B_harmonic = 0
